author: Robert Carr
description: A ciabatta toast with mozzarella, folded prosciutto, a butter-fried egg, and arugula dressed with olive oil and pickle brine.

groceryIngredients.item: ciabatta roll or sturdy hoagie roll

ingredients: 1 small ciabatta roll or 2 slices ciabatta bread, split
1 ounce prosciutto, torn into bite-size pieces
Small handful baby arugula, patted dry
A few very thin onion slices
1 teaspoon pickle brine from the jar
instructions: Stir the mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, and 1/2 teaspoon pickle brine together in a small bowl.
Toss the arugula, onion slices, chopped pickles, remaining 1/2 teaspoon pickle brine, and 1/2 teaspoon extra-virgin olive oil together with your fingers.
Toast the ciabatta cut-side down in a dry skillet over medium heat for 2 to 4 minutes, until crisp at the edges.
Brush or drizzle the toasted cut sides with the remaining 1/2 teaspoon extra-virgin olive oil.
Spread the Dijon mayo lightly over the warm toast and add the mozzarella so it softens slightly.
Melt the butter in the skillet over medium-low heat.
Crack in the egg and cook until the white is set and the yolk is as soft or firm as you like, 2 to 3 minutes for a runny yolk or 4 to 5 minutes for a firmer yolk.
Drape the prosciutto over the mozzarella in loose folds.
Add the fried egg and pile the dressed arugula mixture over or alongside it.
Eat open-faced with a knife and fork, or close it carefully if the bread pieces match.
notes: Egg, butter, and olive oil make the toast more filling while keeping it simple.
The butter-fried egg adds richness; olive oil and pickle brine keep the arugula bright.
Fold the prosciutto loosely so each bite pulls away cleanly.
For less mess, cook the yolk firmer or break it before closing the sandwich.
title: Prosciutto Mozzarella Fried Egg Toast
